sections.heading: 🌍 Global Market Briefing — Asian Session
sections.content: Risk sentiment remains cautiously optimistic in early Asian trading as markets digest elevated oil prices and persistent USD strength. The Dollar's resilience continues to pressure emerging market currencies and commodities, while equity markets show mixed performance amid rotation concerns. Central bank policy divergence remains the dominant macro theme, with Fed pause expectations contrasting sharply with ECB dovishness.

sections.heading: 📊 FOREX — Currency Strength
sections.content:
Strongest to Weakest (Intraday): USD (+0.33% avg) > GBP (-0.19%) > NZD (-0.13%) > EUR (-0.22%) > AUD (-0.28%)
The Dollar Index maintains bullish momentum across major pairs, benefiting from yield differentials and safe-haven demand. Commodity currencies underperform as AUD and CAD weaken on China growth concerns despite higher oil prices.

sections.heading: 💱 Key Currency Pairs
sections.content:
EUR/USD 1.1761 (-0.22%): Bearish bias intact below 1.1800 resistance. ECB dovish pivot weighs on EUR with US data strength supporting further downside toward 1.1700 support.
USD/JPY 157.6180 (+0.32%): Intervention risk elevated above 157.50 but carry trade dynamics support higher levels. Watch for BoJ verbal warnings near 158.00.
GBP/USD 1.3583 (-0.19%): Range-bound between 1.3550-1.3620. BoE policy uncertainty caps upside despite relative Sterling resilience.

sections.heading: 💰 FX Flows & Positioning
sections.content:
• Heavy USD buying from real money accounts across majors
• Commodity currency shorts building on China demand concerns
• JPY intervention hedges increasing as USD/JPY approaches 158
• Month-end rebalancing flows favor USD strength continuation

sections.heading: 📈 EQUITIES — Indices
sections.content:
Top Performers: Nikkei 225 62,709 (+0.47%) > FTSE 100 10,269 (+0.36%) > S&P 500 5,739.30 (+0.23%)
Laggards: CAC 40 8,056 (-0.69%) leads decline
Japanese equities benefit from weaker Yen competitiveness while European markets diverge. Technology sector rotation continues as Nasdaq underperforms broader S&P 500. Defensive positioning evident in utilities and healthcare outperformance.

sections.heading: 🛢️ COMMODITIES
sections.content:
Gold 4,729.10 (+0.01%): Consolidating near record highs. Central bank buying supports 4,700 floor with 4,750 resistance.
WTI Crude 99.15 (+1.10%): Breaking above 99 signals potential move toward 102. Geopolitical premium and inventory draws bullish.
Silver 86.77 (+0.95%): Industrial demand narrative strengthening above 86 support.
Natural Gas 2.925 (+0.52%): Weather-driven demand supporting near-term upside.

sections.heading: ₿ CRYPTO
sections.content:
BTC: 81,020 (-0.86% / -$702) — Testing 80,500 support after rejection at 82,000 resistance. Institutional flows remain constructive despite near-term weakness.
ETH: 2,308.36 (-1.33% / -$31) — ETH/BTC ratio at 0.0285 showing relative weakness. Key support at 2,280 critical for trend continuation.
Alt Performance: XRP 1.4593 (-1.19%), SOL 96.15 (-1.23%), DOGE 0.1100 (-1.11%) all declining with broad crypto risk-off.

sections.heading: 📈 LONG
sections.content:
USD/CHF above 0.7800 — SNB dovishness, USD strength
WTI Crude on pullbacks to 98.50 — Supply concerns persist
Nikkei futures — Yen weakness benefits exporters
Natural Gas above 2.90 — Weather demand uptick
📉 SHORT
EUR/USD below 1.1750 — ECB dovish pivot accelerating
AUD/USD on rallies to 0.7260 — China demand concerns
CAC 40 futures — French political uncertainty weighs
BTC below 80,500 — Technical breakdown risk

sections.heading: 📅 Today's Macro Drivers
sections.content: US Empire State Manufacturing Index (13:30 CET), ECB's Lagarde speech (15:00 CET), China property data overnight. Monitor 10Y Treasury yields above 4.65% for USD continuation signals.
